content.problemStatement:
Alice has two objects:


A cuboid with length AAA units, height BBB units, and width CCC units.
A cube with an edge length of XXX units.
Alice wants to know which of the two objects has a larger volume, or if their volumes are equal.


Input Format

The first and only line of input will contain four space-separated integers AAA, BBB, CCC and XXX, the length of the cuboid, the width of the cuboid, the height of the cuboid, and the length of the edge of the cube.


Output Format
Print a single line containing the string:



`"Cuboid"`, if the volume of the cuboid is greater than the volume of the cube.
`"Cube"`, if the volume of the cube is greater than the volume of the cuboid.
`"Equal"`, if both objects have equal volume.

Print the string without quotes.

You can print each character of the output in either uppercase or lowercase.

For example, the strings `Cube`, `CUBE`, `cube`, and `CuBe` are considered identical.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The volume of the cuboid is 1×3×10=301\times 3\times 10 = 301×3×10=30 cubic units.
The volume of the cube is 33=273^3 = 2733=27 cubic units.
The cuboid has larger volume.

Explanation:
The volume of the cuboid is 1×1×1=11\times 1\times 1 = 11×1×1=1 cubic units.
The volume of the cube is 23=82^3 = 823=8 cubic units.
The cube has larger volume.

Explanation:
The volume of the cuboid is 8×4×2=648\times 4\times 2 = 648×4×2=64 cubic units.
The volume of the cube is 43=644^3 = 6443=64 cubic units.
Both objects have the same volume.
